subject: Math

game_theme.title: Coordinate Plane and Graphing
game_theme.mission_intro: Use graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships to solve real Grade 5 math missions.

lesson.objective: graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships
lesson.concept_explanation: graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships means reading the situation, matching it to a visual model, and using precise Grade 5 math language before answering.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is focusing on one number or one visible feature without checking the labels, units, axes, or attributes that give the problem meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: Students, scientists, designers, builders, and data readers use these skills to describe and solve real problems clearly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model, name the important labels, and answer with accurate origin, x-axis, y-axis, ordered pairs, coordinates, patterns, graph relationships.

page_audio.text: This mini lesson introduces graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships. The visual model is part of the thinking, not decoration. Say the important labels aloud, then solve with the correct math language.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Plot an ordered pair
worked_examples.text: For (4, 3), start at the origin, move 4 units on the x-axis, then 3 units up on the y-axis.

page_audio.text: An ordered pair is written x comma y. The x-coordinate tells how far to move right from the origin. The y-coordinate tells how far to move up.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.title: Read a graph pattern
worked_examples.text: A table and graph can show the same rule. If y is two times x, the point for x equals 3 is (3, 6).

page_audio.text: A graph pattern connects table rows to plotted points. Each input becomes the x-coordinate. Each output becomes the y-coordinate.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

hint_ladder: Read the question carefully and name the math idea.
Use the visual model to check each part before answering.
Explain why the answer matches the units, graph, or attributes.

Use the visual model to check each part before answering.
Explain why the answer matches the units, graph, or attributes.

feedback.correct: Yes! Your answer matches the model and the math relationship.

story.text: Welcome to Coordinate Plane and Graphing. In this mission, you will use graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships to solve problems that feel like real classroom and world tasks. Watch how the labels, units, and visual model tell you what to do next.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships. Read the model before calculating, because the model shows what each number or attribute means. Use precise math language: origin, x-axis, y-axis, ordered pairs, coordinates, patterns, graph relationships.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: On the Watch page, slow down and follow one complete example. First name what the question asks, then connect each label in the visual to the operation or property. Finally, say the answer in a complete math sentence.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: On the Demo page, build the reasoning with support. Notice where common mistakes happen, such as reversing coordinates, converting the wrong direction, or using only one shape attribute. The model is here to help you catch the mistake early.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This Practice page lets you try the skill yourself. Tap Read Question before you answer so the measurement, coordinate, or geometry words are spoken clearly. Then use the visual and choose or type the answer carefully.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This Challenge page may combine ideas or ask for a deeper explanation. Tap Read Question, study the visual, and decide which information matters most. If the answer has a unit, coordinate, or shape category, include it.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This Checkpoint page checks what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, solve without rushing, and compare your answer to the model. Your response should show that you understand the graph first-quadrant points and interpret coordinate relationships.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You reached the badge page for Coordinate Plane and Graphing. Think about the model that helped most and the misconception you avoided. This badge celebrates careful reasoning, not just a quick answer.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: The profile page saves your growth for Coordinate Plane and Graphing. Review your strengths, then continue to the Skill Practice Center for focused levels or Mixed review. Choose Practice This Skill when you want more questions with Read Question support.
profile.label: Read This Page
